title: Streams, Redirection, Quoting, and Pipelines · Terminal Trace: Quote Rules
summary: Build a repeatable incident-report pipeline whose data and diagnostics remain distinct and whose failures cannot be hidden by downstream success. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux quote rules increment in a stream-routing and pipeline-trace workbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply unquoted, single-quoted, double-quoted, escaped, and ANSI-C quoted contexts to preserve intended arguments.
Predict truncating, appending, input, descriptor duplication, and combined redirection from left-to-right order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Quote Rules
steps.instruction:
theory case — A 4-record incident command writes selected rows to report.txt and diagnostics to report.err. One grep stage finds no matches; predict descriptor routing and final status with pipefail before running.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Quote Rules contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for apply unquoted, single-quoted, double-quoted, escaped, and ansi-c quoted contexts to preserve intended arguments.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Capture identity, path, process, file, and service state; predict parsing and scope; run one safe simulated observation; interpret exit status; then change one bounded layer and verify rollback. Apply that move specifically to Quote Rules while producing a stream-routing and pipeline-trace workbook.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A 4-record incident command writes selected rows to report.txt and diagnostics to report.err. One grep stage finds no matches; predict descriptor routing and final status with pipefail before running. Competency probe: Apply unquoted, single-quoted, double-quoted, escaped, and ANSI-C quoted contexts to preserve intended arguments. A defensible solution applies Quote Rules, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a stream-routing and pipeline-trace workbook.

content.code:
# Worked focus: linux-quote-rules — Apply unquoted, single-quoted, double-quoted, escaped, and ANSI-C quoted contexts to preserve intended arguments.
set -o pipefail
grep -E '^INC-[0-9]+,high,' data/incidents.csv \
  >report.txt 2>report.err
status=$?
printf 'pipeline_status=%d\n' "$status"
content.caption: Runnable safe terminal evidence example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
